Title: The transcriptional landscape and dynamics regulating organ differentiation and dormancy in Curcuma alismatifolia

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ABA at the 8' position to promote ABA degradation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: reduces target product by catalyzing its catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for dormancy release
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in buds and rhizomes related to sprouting; increased in ungerminated buds under high temperature and decreased after sprouting elongation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis overexpression lines, transgene expression level correlated with faster seed germination under ABA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature (30 °C) promoted rhizome dormancy release; ABA-containing medium (0, 0.2, 0.5 μM) used in Arabidopsis germination assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression; native CYP707A1 promoter used in dual-LUC/Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA degradation and rhizome dormancy release; accelerates Arabidopsis seed germination and early flowering when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Temporal transcriptome analysis; phylogenetic analysis; RT-qPCR under temperature treatments; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; seed germination assays; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP707A1 was identified among sprouting-associated P450 genes enriched during rhizome dormancy release. 2. Its expression increased in high-temperature ungerminated buds and declined after sprouting, supporting a role in early dormancy release. 3. Overexpression of Curcuma CYP707A1 in Arabidopsis accelerated germination and flowering, consistent with enhanced ABA catabolism. 4. MYB96, WRKY35, RAV1, and ARR18 regulate the CYP707A1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to accelerate rhizome dormancy release and enable year-round production; could be manipulated together with upstream TFs and temperature management
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in Arabidopsis caused reduced height, fewer and smaller rosette leaves, and earlier flowering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The allele Chr07HB1157 was selected for heterologous validation because both haplotype alleles showed similar expression and only minor amino-acid differences.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote bud elongation, growth, and dormancy release-related development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-beta-hydroxylation/deactivation of bioactive or precursor gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: reduces active GA pool affecting bud elongation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in ungerminated buds but downregulated in sprouting buds under high temperature
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in Arabidopsis correlated with reduced plant height at flowering; no major seed germination effect
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature (30 °C); ABA in Arabidopsis germination assays; exogenous GA treatment discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Primarily contributes to bud elongation after dormancy release rather than initiating dormancy release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during sprouting/high-temperature treatment; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; seed germination and plant height phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2ox1 was one of six dormancy-related candidate genes showing sprouting-state expression changes. 2. Its expression was high in ungerminated buds and decreased in sprouting buds, suggesting involvement after dormancy release. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis reduced plant height but did not alter seed germination, indicating a role in elongation/growth rather than dormancy breaking.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate post-dormancy bud elongation and plant stature, but less suitable than CYP707A1 for triggering dormancy release
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused reduced plant height and late flowering-like growth effects in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes GA2ox1 is not a primary regulator of rhizome dormancy release in C. alismatifolia.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and regulates sprouting responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA catabolism linked to rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream regulator contributing to control of ABA degradation and dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High temperature and dormancy-release state context; ABA-related regulation inferred
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected based on congruent expression with CYP707A1 during dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in dual-LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome dormancy release context; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector expressed from pGreenII 62-SK; target promoter was native CYP707A1 promoter in reporter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates CYP707A1 promoter and thereby is implicated in promoting rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression filtering; promoter binding-site prediction; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB96 was identified as a candidate upstream TF of CYP707A1 based on interaction prediction, promoter motif presence, and expression coherence. 2. It regulated CYP707A1 promoter activity in tobacco leaves. 3. Y1H supported binding to the CYP707A1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target to tune CYP707A1 and dormancy release without direct editing of catabolic enzyme
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direction of activation or repression was not explicitly stated in the text.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Curcuma alismatifolia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and regulates sprouting responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA catabolism linked to dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates promoter activity of CYP707A1; binds CYP707A1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Validated direct regulator of the key ABA catabolism gene controlling dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected for expression coherence with CYP707A1 during dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome dormancy release context; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector expressed from pGreenII 62-SK; target promoter was native CYP707A1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly regulates CYP707A1 and is implicated in rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression filtering; promoter binding-site prediction; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY35 was prioritized as a candidate regulator of CYP707A1. 2. Dual-LUC showed WRKY35 altered CYP707A1 promoter activity. 3. Y1H demonstrated direct binding to the CYP707A1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream regulator for modifying dormancy release timing through CYP707A1 control
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text specifically notes WRKY factor binding validation to CYP707A1 promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Curcuma alismatifolia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and regulates sprouting responses

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates promoter activity of CYP707A1; binds CYP707A1 promoter

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected for expression congruence with CYP707A1 during dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome dormancy release context; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector expressed from pGreenII 62-SK; target promoter was native CYP707A1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates CYP707A1 and is implicated in promoting rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression filtering; promoter binding-site prediction; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAV1 was identified as an upstream TF candidate for CYP707A1. 2. Dual-LUC showed RAV1 affected CYP707A1 promoter activity. 3. Y1H supported direct promoter binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate ABA catabolism and dormancy-release timing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direction of regulation was not explicitly assigned in the main text.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Curcuma alismatifolia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and regulates sprouting responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA catabolism linked to rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates promoter activity of CYP707A1; binds CYP707A1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate upstream regulator of the key ABA catabolism gene associated with dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected based on expression pattern matching CYP707A1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome dormancy release context; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector expressed from pGreenII 62-SK; target promoter was native CYP707A1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates CYP707A1 and is implicated in rhizome dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression filtering; promoter binding-site prediction; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR18 was added as an expression-supported candidate regulator of CYP707A1. 2. Dual-LUC showed ARR18 affected CYP707A1 promoter activity. 3. Y1H supported binding to the CYP707A1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream node for engineering dormancy release through CYP707A1 regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This factor was chosen additionally based on expression pattern rather than STRING prediction.
